Title: Fix/Implement duplicate product detection flow and repair 'Proceed Anyway' button
Description: Client expects duplicate detection when creating new products. Additionally, the 'Proceed Anyway' button in the Create New Product modal is not working and blocks the form from processing.
Acceptance Criteria:
- If duplicate check is enabled: system detects potential duplicates and presents a confirm modal.
- 'Proceed Anyway' successfully submits and creates the product.
- If duplicate check is not intended: remove/disable the duplicate warning UI (ASSUMED) to eliminate the broken Proceed flow.
- Add automated test covering the Proceed flow.
Notes/Dependencies: ASSUMED: Duplicate detection is intended ('Was this page supposed to have a check for duplicate entry?'). Confirm requirement; implement accordingly.
Hunter Development Notes:
Status update 2026-03-02: 'Proceed anyway' still re-runs dedupe and blocks; needs override/confirm flow decision + implementation (components/product-create-modal.tsx).
Today the app shows “Proceed anyway,” but clicking it just re-checks for similar products and then stops again. So it feels like the button “does nothing.”

Here are a few different approaches we can take to resolve the issue, and what the user experience would look like:

Option A : “Proceed anyway” really proceeds
- User clicks Save → sees a list of possible duplicates.
- User clicks “Proceed anyway” → the product is created even though similar ones exist.
- Still blocked only for a true exact duplicate (same exact name/part #), if you want that safety.

Option B: “Proceed anyway” becomes “I understand, create it anyway”
- Same as Option A, but with an extra confirmation step (or a checkbox).
- This reduces accidental duplicates because users have to explicitly acknowledge the warning.

Option C (Reduce noisy warnings): warn less often so “Proceed anyway” is rarely needed
- The system becomes pickier about what counts as “possible duplicate” (for example: only warn if the same vendor/distributor matches, or if the part # matches exactly).
- Result: users see fewer “Possible duplicates” banners; when they do see it, it’s more likely to be a real conflict.

Option D (Policy/permissions): only certain people can “Proceed anyway”
- Regular users must pick an existing product or change details.
- Managers/admins can override and create anyway (with logging).
- Result: fewer duplicates overall, but some users may need help/escalation to create new products.

Let me know your preference on how we want to handle this and I can get it done ASAP.
